At an age when she's supposed to paint her childhood with laughter and games,
Farah, a 9-year-old Palestinian girl from Gaza, spent her time writing her will in decorations and drawings.
She didn't write about school, or toys, or a beautiful tomorrow,
but wrote about what she fears, what she misses, and what she wishes would remain afterward.

Urgent Humanitarian Appeal :

We urge the world to open an urgent humanitarian corridor and help Mohamed now.

هأرتس: مؤمن مقداد  
نكشف اعتمادا على صور أقمار صناعية حديثة عن تغيّرات ميدانية واسعة في قطاع غزة بعد ثلاثة أشهر من إعلان انتهاء القتال. وتظهر الصور ترسخ انتشار الجيش الإسرائيلي على طول ما يعرف بـ"الخط الأصفر"، الذي يتوغل أحيانا مئات الأمتار داخل مناطق تعدّ رسميا تحت سيطرة حم/س.

كما توثق الصور توسع بؤر الدمار حتى خلال وقف إطلاق النار، لا سيما في جباليا والشجاعية، إضافة إلى ظهور مجمعات خيام جديدة في مناطق أخرى لإيواء عشرات آلاف النازحين. وتشير المعطيات من الصور إلى سيطرة إسرائيلية على نحو 54% من مساحة القطاع، مع تغييرات واضحة في أنماط السيطرة والأنشطة على الأرض.

وتعتمد التحقيقات على صور التقطتها شركة بلانت لابس، مدعومة بتحليل، والتي تظهر إقامة 13 موقعا عسكريا جديدا منذ وقف إطلاق النار، بينها مواقع مرتفعة في جباليا أُنشئت عبر هدم مبان وتجريف واسع.

كما تكشف الصور استمرار هدم مئات المباني قرب المستشفى الإندونيسي، بعضها غرب الخط الأصفر، مع اختلافات ملحوظة بين مسار الخط كما تعلنه إسرائيل ومواقع الكتل الصفراء على الأرض، بما في ذلك في الشجاعية حيث تظهر الكتل على بعد نحو 300 متر غرب الخط المعلن.

The Palestinian Ministry of Health in Gaza reported on January 15, 2026, that the daily toll for the past 24 hours was 2 martyrs and 5 injuries, bringing the total number of casualties since October 7, 2023, to 71,441 martyrs and 171,329 injuries. The report notes that victims remain under rubble and inaccessible to rescue crews. Furthermore, since the ceasefire that began on October 11, the total has reached 451 martyrs, 1,251 injuries, and 710 retrievals of bodies.

Amid the rubble, humanity persists.
The film captures everyday stories of resilience: a barber who keeps welcoming his clients despite the destruction, restoring dignity and a fleeting moment of beauty; and Wafa, a woman who has devoted her life to children with disabilities or those who have lost their families, offering them care, education, play, and a sense of normalcy in the midst of chaos.
This is more than a film.
It is an intimate portrait of the strength of communities living in one of the world's most fragile and devastated places-where humanity continues to endure.

The health condition of freed journalist Mujahid Bani Mufleh has sharply deteriorated, after he was transferred to hospital in critical condition and diagnosed with a brain hemorrhage, shortly following his release from Israeli prisons.

The deterioration is the result of deliberate medical neglect, including the denial of essential medications for diabetes and hypertension, followed by the administration of irregular dosages, which caused a dangerous disruption in his blood pressure levels. Clear signs of beating and torture have also been documented on his body, particularly to the head and limbs, leading to loss of sensation in his hands and a noticeable limp caused by tight restraints.

Bani Mufleh underwent surgery earlier today, which was reported successful. He remains in need of continued medical care and close monitoring to fully recover.

ويأتي هذا التدهور نتيجة إهمال طبي متعمد، تمثل بحرمانه من أدويته الأساسية لمرضَي السكري والضغط، ثم تزويده لاحقاً بجرعات غير منتظمة، ما تسبب بخلل خطير في مستويات ضغط الدم. كما تظهر على جسده آثار ضرب وتعذيب خاصة في الرأس والأطراف، أدت إلى فقدان الإحساس في يديه وعرج واضح في مشيته نتيجة القيود المشددة.

Misk.. When fingertips see what eyes cannot.
With unwavering will and from the heart of war's ruins, 11-year-old Misk challenged her visual impairment to create the most beautiful handmade accessories. Misk didn't just craft jewelry; she crafted a lesson in hope: that light radiates from within, and that surrender simply does not exist in the dictionary of creators.

President Trump's latest appearance was a whirlwind of humor, high-stakes diplomacy, and a surprising moment of exhaustion:

The Milk Prank: While signing a bill to bring whole milk back to schools, Trump joked with reporters about a 5-day-old bottle of milk, saying:
"I brought it so the press can have some. You can swig it all."

﻿﻿Political Sparring: The mood shifted as he fielded questions on Greenland. When asked about potential force or options, he fired back: "I wouldn't be telling YOU."

﻿﻿Global Updates: On Iran, Trump noted he was informed that killings and executions had stopped, adding: "We will find out about it."

﻿﻿The "Stunning" Moment: Despite the intense discussions, the event took a turn when Trump appeared to fall asleep during the public meeting, sparking immediate viral reactions.

Urgent Humanitarian Appeal to Save the Child Shahd Al-Baz (6 years old)
Shahd can no longer bear to look at herself and is subjected to harsh bullying by other children after suffering severe burns and disfigurement to her neck, shoulders, and face as a result of an Israeli attack.
She endures constant physical pain and recurring infections, along with profound psychological distress that has robbed her of her childhood.
Due to the severe lack of medical resources in Gaza, doctors have urgently recommended transferring her abroad to receive reconstructive and cosmetic surgeries.
Save Shahd... give her back her childhood and put an end to her pain.

The final text of the “Death Penalty for Palestinian Prisoners” bill, spearheaded by National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, has been released. The bill stipulates that the sentence be carried out by hanging at the hands of a specially appointed prison guard, whose identity will remain confidential and who will be granted full criminal immunity.
According to the draft, prisoners will be held in a separate facility with visits restricted to authorized entities only. Communication with lawyers will be conducted via video call, without any direct physical presence.
The bill also mandates that the execution be carried out within 90 days of its issuance, in the presence of the prison director, a representative of the judicial authority, an official observer, and a representative of the prisoner’s family.
